decks.notes: This deck revolves around using Doomsday as the primary win condition, with various utility cards and counter spells to protect the combo. The deck aims to set up a winning pile of cards using Doomsday, while also having interaction and protection against opponent's spells. The deck includes some unique card choices like "Lórien Revealed" and "The One Ring", along with versatile cards like "Teferi, Time Raveler" and "Veil of Summer". By carefully planning out plays and utilizing the combo effectively, this deck can quickly secure a victory.

Utilize graveyard recursion and fast mana to bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa quickly and dominate the game. Use disruptive spells like Thoughtseize, Unmask, and Collective Brutality to clear the way for your combo. Take advantage of efficient reanimation spells like Animate Dead and Reanimate to cheat out creatures and overwhelm your opponent. Use Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers to disrupt your opponent's strategy and Cabal Therapy to further disrupt their hand. Use the versatile mana base to support your powerful spells and ensure consistent performance.

The deck focuses on utilizing lands as control and disruption elements to outlast opponents and ultimately win the game. It relies on strong land cards like Wasteland, The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale, and Urza's Saga, along with key support cards such as Life from the Loam and Crop Rotation to manipulate the battlefield and slow down the opponent. By efficiently managing resources and utilizing lands effectively, the deck aims to create a strategic advantage and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of disruptive elements and powerful threats.

The deck focuses on assembling the Painter's Servant and Grindstone combo to mill the opponent's library. It also utilizes Goblin Engineer and Goblin Welder to search for key artifacts and manipulate the board state. The deck includes various support cards like Pyroblast and Ensnaring Bridge to control the game and protect the combo. Additionally, Urza's Saga helps to tutor for specific artifacts while Ancient Tomb provides ramp. Overall, the strategy revolves around setting up the combo pieces quickly and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck is focused on utilizing the Death & Taxes archetype to disrupt opponents while applying pressure with aggressive creatures. Key cards include Stoneforge Mystic to fetch equipment like Swords to Plowshares, Skyclave Apparition for removal, and Aether Vial for efficient creature deployment. Recruiter of the Guard provides toolbox options, while Yorion, Sky Nomad acts as a powerful companion. The deck aims to control the board with disruptive elements like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, and Thalia, Heretic Cathar, while closing out the game with powerful finishers like Solitude and Overlord of the Balemurk.

The deck focuses on cheating out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Archon of Cruelty early in the game using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It also includes powerful support cards like Force of Will and Brainstorm to protect the combo pieces and dig for the key cards. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and disrupt their game plan with counterspells and disruptive cards like Stock Up. The strategy revolves around quickly assembling the combo pieces and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent's resources and controlling the game through artifacts. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere, Karn, the Great Creator, and Ugin, Eye of the Storm, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and limit their options. The inclusion of cards like Grim Monolith and Urza's Saga helps ramp up mana production and generate advantage. The ultimate goal is to establish a powerful board presence with Artifacts Prison strategy and eventually overwhelm the opponent with threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Kozilek's Command.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources through various interactive spells like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Stifle while also disrupting their land base with cards like Wasteland. The deck also includes powerful card draw and selection options with Ponder, Brainstorm, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to ensure consistency. Additionally, the deck includes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to provide additional control elements and win conditions. The goal is to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent while eventually overwhelming them with powerful threats.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize Eldrazi creatures and disrupt the opponent's strategies with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. By combining aggressive creatures like Reality Smasher and Eldrazi Obligator with disruptive spells such as Kozilek's Command, the deck aims to pressure the opponent while maintaining control of the game. With powerful mana acceleration from cards like Cavern of Souls and Lotus Petal, this deck can quickly overwhelm opponents and close out games before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to control the pace of the game with disruptive spells like Force of Will and Daze, while also using aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Dragon's Rage Channeler to pressure the opponent. The deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot with efficient removal like Lightning Bolt and tempo plays like bouncing threats with Brazen Borrower. The use of cards like Mishra's Bauble and Preordain allow for card selection and potential card advantage, while maintaining a consistent mana base with fetch lands and utility lands like Wasteland.

This UWx Control deck focuses on disrupting opponents with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze, along with versatile removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending. The deck also utilizes disruptive cards like Stifle and Wasteland to slow down opponents' mana development. The deck's win conditions revolve around powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Teferi, Time Raveler, along with efficient creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The overall strategy is to control the game, disrupt the opponent's plans, and ultimately outvalue them with powerful spells and threats.

The deck focuses on using mana acceleration and strong creature threats to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. It aims to ramp up quickly using cards like Gaea's Cradle and Natural Order to bring out powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth. The deck also includes disruption elements like Wasteland and Collector Ouphe to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and win the game through a combination of large creatures and efficient mana usage.

This deck focuses on utilizing disruptive spells like Force of Will, Thoughtseize, and Daze to control the opponent's plays while deploying aggressive threats like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters to quickly close out the game. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and flexibility, while cards like Fatal Push and Snuff Out offer efficient removal options. Wasteland helps to disrupt the opponent's mana base, while the inclusion of a couple of finishers like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Brazen Borrower ensures the deck can adapt to different game states and strategies.

This Grixis Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler, Murktide Regent, and Nethergoyf, backed up by disruption spells like Daze and Force of Will. The deck can also generate card advantage and filter through the deck with cards like Mishra's Bauble and Brainstorm. The inclusion of Lightning Bolt and Fatal Push provides removal options for dealing with threats, while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Proft's Eidetic Memory offer additional card advantage and utility. The deck also includes land disruption with Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on playing aggressively with creatures that provide strong defense and powerful effects, while utilizing cards like Magus of the Moon and Blood Moon to disrupt opponent's mana bases. The deck aims to control the board and apply pressure quickly, taking advantage of its ability to ramp and lock down non-basic lands. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere and Chancellor of the Annex, the deck aims to establish a dominant position in the early game and maintain control throughout the match.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Griselbrand, then bring them back to the battlefield using cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead. This combo-based strategy aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats early in the game, while also using disruptive cards like Unmask and Force of Will to protect the combo. The deck also includes cards like Faithless Looting and Brainstorm to help dig for combo pieces and enhance card draw. Overall, the strategy revolves around quickly reanimating powerful creatures to dominate the game.
